thread.title: Hill sworn in as Grayson County DA | Texas | tylerpaper.com
thread.title_full: Hill sworn in as Grayson County DA | Texas | tylerpaper.com

author: Abby Haymond
title: Hill sworn in as Grayson County DA | Texas | tylerpaper.com
text:
SHERMAN, Texas (KTEN) — Grayson County has a new district attorney.
John Kermit Hill was sworn in Friday morning, and has already started checking things off his docket.
Former DA Brett Smith, who lost a reelection bid, stepped down early in October. County commissioners voted to let Hill start his duties early.
"We don't need to have a defendant sitting, waiting for trial for four years. And, there's at least one case that that's the case right now," said Hill.
Along with Hill, five new assistant district attorneys were sworn in, joining the eight who chose to stay in the office and join Hill's team after Smith's departure.
"They're able to step in right now and help us get rid of that backlog of cases," Hill said. "Then we need to be in a position that when — unfortunately — more of these cases arise, we're able to just take care of the situation."
Hill said he is eager to get the ball rolling and hopefully help clear out some of the county's overpopulated jail.
The new district attorney said his focus will be on crimes against children and violent crime cases.
KTEN.com
Originally published on kten.com , part of the BLOX Digital Content Exchange .
